DISCOVERY 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS-COMPLAINANTS' DUFF AND DOWLING'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS NO. 1 & 3 IS GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT CROSS-DEFENDANTS CLAIM THAT IT HAS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED ALL SUCH DOCUMENTS IT SHALL PRODUCE FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANTS INSPECTION A COPY OF THE SEARCH PROTOCOL TO ESTABLISH THAT THE SEARCH CRITERIA WAS BROAD ENOUGH TO IDENTIFY ALL DOCUMENTS REQUESTED BY REQUESTS NO. 1 & 3. COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER AND SUBMIT TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON NOV. 5, 2012. ORDER SIGNED. PAUL RENNE, JUDGE PRO-TEM, JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, CLERK, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANTS WHITE OAK HOLDINGS, LLC, WHITE OAK PARTNERS LLC, WHITE OAK FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC AND WHITE OAK MERCHANT PARTNERS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION, AND SUSTAINED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HAVE PLED FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION UNDER THE OPERATING AGREEMENT; MOVING PARTY'S SUGGESTION (MEMORANDUM OF POINTS & AUTHORITIES AT 6 LINE 21) THAT THE CROSS COMPLAINT (XC) DOES NOT PLEAD AN ENFORCEABLE CONTRACT IS INCORRECT. COMPARE, E.G., XC AT 28, 48. SEE ALSO DEL. C. 18-101(7)(B). CROSS-COMPLAINANTS REQUEST LEAVE TO AMEND THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: CROSS COMPLAINANTS PHILIP DUFF, PATRICK DOWLING'S MOTION TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT CONDITIONS. CROSS-DEFENDANTS FAIL TO SHOW THAT THEY WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION AND THUS THE STRONG POLICY FAVORING THE LIBERAL GRANTING OF AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS CONTROLS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-11-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANTs PHILIP DUFF, PATRICK DOWLING's Motion To File Second Amended Complaint is granted without conditions. Cross-Defendants fail to show that they would be prejudiced by the granting of this motion and thus the strong policy favoring the liberal granting of amendments to pleadings controls. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-22-2012 Continued To Jun-01-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
CROSS DEFENDANTS WHITE OAK HOLDINGS, LLC, WHITE OAK PARTNERS LLC, WHITE OAK FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC and WHITE OAK MERCHANT PARTNERS, LLC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Overruled as to the first and fifth causes of action, and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second cause of action. Cross-Complainants have pled facts sufficient to allege causes of action under the operating agreement; moving party's suggestion (Memorandum of Points & Authorities at 6 line 21) that the cross complaint (XC) does not plead an enforceable contract is incorrect. Compare, e.g., XC at 28, 48. See also Del. C. 18-101(7)(b). Cross-Complainants request leave to amend the allegations of the second cause of action, granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Curtis Karnow, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/CK)
